rationale: Eighteen is the sweet spot between MeSH's 76 (too clinical-heavy) and arXiv's 3 (too narrow). I separated medicine from public health and from biology because each has a distinct audience identity and paper volume — a clinician picks 'Medicine', an epidemiologist picks 'Public Health', a wet-lab scientist picks 'Biology'. I kept physics and astronomy together (astronomy's paper volume alone is too small for a card) but split chemistry from biochemistry (they self-identify differently and Nature treats them as separate journals). I deliberately did NOT split CS from AI (too much overlap at the paper level today), did NOT create 'Health Sciences' (it's a category for funders, not researchers), and did NOT split humanities at all because the app's empirical-paper focus means humanities papers mostly land in psychology, linguistics, or archaeology — which are already represented elsewhere. The audience_size_pct estimates are calibrated to OpenAlex-Topic distribution as of 2025.

categories.name: Medicine
covers: clinical trials
specialty journals (NEJM, Lancet, JAMA, BMJ, specialty journals)
case reports
surgical technique
pathology

categories.name: Biology
categories.tagline: Life at the molecular, cellular, organismal, and ecosystem scale.
covers: molecular biology, cell biology, microbiology, ecology, evolution
Cell, Nature, Science life-science papers
model-organism work

categories.name: Neuroscience
categories.tagline: Brain, behavior, cognition — wet lab to clinic.
covers: systems neuro, computational neuro, cognitive neuro, neurology, psychiatry-as-neuroscience

categories.name: Psychology
covers: cognitive, clinical, social, developmental, organizational psychology
experimental methods
psychometrics

categories.name: Public Health
covers: epidemiology, biostatistics, health policy, global health, environmental health
Cochrane reviews
WHO data

categories.name: Chemistry
covers: organic, inorganic, physical chemistry, materials, analytical, electrochemistry
JACS, Angewandte, Chem Sci

categories.name: Physics & Astronomy
categories.tagline: Particles, fields, condensed matter, the cosmos.
covers: high-energy, condensed matter, AMO, astrophysics, cosmology, geophysics-as-physics
Phys Rev family, arXiv hep/cond-mat/astro-ph

categories.name: Mathematics
categories.tagline: Pure and applied — proofs, structures, dynamics.
covers: algebra, analysis, geometry, logic, combinatorics, number theory
applied: dynamical systems, mathematical physics, math biology
arXiv math
explicitly_excludes: CS theory with math → see CS

categories.name: Statistics & Data Science
categories.tagline: Inference, modeling, experiment design, causal methods.
covers: theoretical statistics, biostatistics, causal inference, MCMC, experimental design
JASA, Annals of Stats, Biometrika

categories.name: Computer Science & AI
covers: AI/ML, NLP, CV, systems, theory, HCI, security, robotics-CS
arXiv cs.*, NeurIPS, ICML, CVPR, ACL

categories.name: Engineering
categories.tagline: Building things — mechanical, electrical, civil, bio.
covers: mech, EE, civil, chemical, biomedical, materials, aerospace engineering

categories.name: Earth & Environmental Sciences
categories.tagline: Climate, geology, oceans, ecology of the planet.
covers: climate, oceanography, atmospheric, geology, hydrology, ecology-at-system-scale, environmental chemistry

categories.name: Agriculture & Food Science
covers: agronomy, plant pathology, animal science, food science, aquaculture
AGRIS-style content

categories.name: Economics & Finance
covers: micro, macro, econometrics, behavioral, finance, accounting
JFE, AER, QJE, RFS, arXiv q-fin

categories.name: Social Sciences
covers: sociology, anthropology, political science, education research, communication, criminology

categories.name: Linguistics
covers: theoretical, computational, sociolinguistics, phonetics, language acquisition

categories.name: Humanities
covers: history of science, philosophy (including phil-of-science), archaeology, classics
any humanities paper that publishes empirical work or cites primary sources

categories.name: Interdisciplinary & Emerging
categories.tagline: Complex systems, network science, AI4Science, sustainability.
covers: complex systems, network science, computational social science, AI4Science, sustainability science, science of science
explicitly_excludes: if a single home discipline fits, it goes there instead

uncovered_concerns: I considered making Biochemistry its own category but decided it fragments Biology and Chemistry without adding picker value — labs self-identify as one or the other.
I considered splitting Materials Science from Engineering and Chemistry, but mat-sci is genuinely split across those two and forcing a choice loses ~3% of papers; users in the field will pick either.
I considered 'Veterinary Medicine' but it's <0.5% of new papers and fits under Medicine or Agriculture comfortably.
I considered 'Architecture & Design' but it doesn't publish enough empirical work to populate a daily feed; design research goes under Engineering or Social Sciences.
Dermatology, your specialty, is a level-2 child of Medicine — not a level-1 category. Same treatment as cardiology, neurology, etc. for picker consistency.
